adds.rationale: Oracle's September 9, 2025 fiscal Q1-26 print disclosed a step-change in remaining performance obligations (RPO ~$455B, up ~4x year over year) driven by multi-year OCI capacity contracts with frontier AI labs; this is a concrete commercial milestone that moves Oracle from a legacy database name into a top-tier AI-infrastructure beneficiary alongside the watchlist's existing NVDA/MSFT/AMZN/TSM exposure.

news_evidence.summary: Oracle reported Q1 FY2026 results on Sep 9, 2025 showing remaining performance obligations jumped to roughly $455B (up ~359% YoY), with management attributing the surge to multi-year OCI cloud-capacity contracts signed with large AI customers; shares closed up ~36% the following session, the biggest single-day move in decades.

Press reports following the Sep 9 print described a roughly $300B, five-year OCI compute commitment from OpenAI as the single largest component of Oracle's new RPO, formalizing Oracle as a top-three AI training/inference cloud alongside Microsoft Azure and AWS.

Oracle Q1-26 capex guide was raised to ~$35B for fiscal 2026 to support the new OCI contracts, with management noting that data-center construction and Nvidia GPU procurement remain the gating constraints - a positive read-through to the watchlist's existing NVDA and VRT positions.

removes.rationale: Palantir is the clearest peak-enthusiasm signal on the current watchlist: by late September 2025 the stock traded at roughly 90-100x forward sales (the highest multiple in the large-cap software universe), sell-side price targets had compressed below spot, and short interest plus insider selling had risen materially - classic late-stage signals the mean-variance optimizer's covariance estimate will not price in; AI exposure is preserved via MSFT/NVDA/AMZN/TSM and the incoming ORCL.

news_evidence.summary: Palantir traded above $170 per share by mid-September 2025 at roughly 90-100x consensus forward revenue, the highest forward-sales multiple in the S&P 500 software sub-industry; multiple sell-side desks (including Jefferies and RBC) reiterated Sell/Underperform ratings with price targets well below spot.

Form 4 filings through Q3-25 showed continued executive and director sales at PLTR (CEO Alex Karp and CTO Shyam Sankar among them), with cumulative 2025 insider sales exceeding $2B - a pattern consistent with late-stage retail-driven enthusiasm rather than fundamental re-rating.

rationale_overall: One AI-for-AI swap at the 16-name cap: remove PLTR on a peak-enthusiasm signal (90-100x forward sales, sell-side targets below spot, sustained insider selling) and add ORCL on a fresh, concrete commercial milestone (the Sep 9 RPO step-change to ~$455B driven by a multi-year OCI deal with OpenAI). Wave-bucket shape is preserved - AI stays at five names (MSFT, NVDA, AMZN, TSM, ORCL), nuclear (CCJ, CEG, SMR) and rockets/spacecraft (RKLB, ITA) and quantum (IONQ) and robotics (ISRG, ROBO) and geopolitical-energy (LNG) and AI-power-infra (VRT) and broad market (SPY) are unchanged on intact theses. The swap rotates from a stretched AI-software name to an AI-infrastructure name whose customer-contract disclosure is one of the largest cloud-capacity bookings on record.
